title: Browser Presigning, POST Policy, and CORS · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A statewide accessible digital library. They need schema-validated upload intents, bounded browser-direct contributions, checksum evidence, quarantine and moderation, accessible progress, CORS separation, exact presigning, and private publication without treating a resolved promise as completed review. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Digital Library Contribution Intake with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, model admitted, uploading, quarantined, scanning, accepted, rejected, published, and deleted transitions with authorized owners.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Upload Byte Time Count Budget
steps.instruction:
project case — A project TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3233 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to finalize a browser upload intent while separating CORS from IAM and promise completion; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Upload Byte Time Count Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
